name: Riera de Castellnou, Salt de la Tosca, Moià – Circular Route
name_original: Riera de Castellnou, Salt de la Tosca, Moià – Ruta Circular
region_intro: Hiking around Moià, the historical and cultural capital of the Moianès region in Catalonia, Spain, offers diverse natural landscapes. Situated at an elevation of over 700 meters, the area features plateaus, valleys, and extensive beech and oak woods. Hikers can explore varied terrain, including natural springs and waterfalls like Salt de la Tosca. The region provides a rich environment for nature enthusiasts. Best hiking trails around Moià The most popular hiking route is Riera de Castellnou, Salt de la Tosca, Moià – Circular Route, a 7.7 miles (12.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 23 minutes to complete. This route allows for experiencing local scenery and a waterfall. Another top favourite among local hikers is Coves del Toll – Collsuspina loop from Cova de les Toixoneres, a moderate 5.6 miles (9.0 km) path. This trail explores the area's cave systems and surrounding landscapes. Local hikers also love the Hermitage of Mare de Déu de la Tosca – Gual riera de Marfà loop from Moià, a 9.6 miles (15.5 km) trail leading through varied terrain including an hermitage, often completed in about 4 hours 19 minutes. Hiking around Moià is defined by plateaus, valleys, and dense forests, with opportunities to discover waterfalls and natural springs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging treks. The routes in Moià are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 90 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Moià's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Moià?
A: There are over 30 hiking routes around Moià, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include 10 easy trails, 19 moderate routes, and 1 challenging trek, ensuring options for various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Moià?
A: Yes, Moià offers several easy trails perfect for beginners or families. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6957575" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Horses in the Fields of Moià – Mas Saiol de Moià loop from Moià&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 5.7 km walk through scenic fields.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in the Moià region?
A: Many of the trails in Moià are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40324130" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Mare de Déu de la Tosca – Gual riera de Marfà loop from Montbrú&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 5.9 km circular route that takes you through varied terrain.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see while hiking in Moià?
A: Moià's landscape is characterized by the Moianès Plateau, featuring plateaus, valleys, and extensive beech and oak woods. You'll also discover beautiful natural springs and impressive waterfalls, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2197382" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt de la Tosca Waterfall and Molí de Brotons&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or attractions to visit along the hiking trails?
A: Absolutely. Hikers can explore several interesting landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/236272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Miquel del Fai Monastery and Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a significant site, known for its stunning waterfall and stalactite caves. You can also visit the charming settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3139217" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collsuspina&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any caves to explore near Moià?
A: Yes, the region is home to interesting cave systems. While hiking, you might encounter caves like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3015059" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balma dels Gobians&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3498609" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Drac de Vilarjoan Cave&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4577635" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Balma d'en Xec&lt;/a&gt;, offering unique opportunities for exploration.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Moià?
A: The komoot community highly rates the hiking experience in Moià, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 90 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the peaceful atmosphere of the forests, and the opportunities to discover natural springs and waterfalls.

Q: What is the typical terrain like on Moià's hiking trails?
A: The terrain around Moià is varied, ranging from the flat expanses of the Moianès Plateau to gentle valleys and dense forests. You'll encounter paths through beech and oak woods, alongside rivers, and sometimes rocky sections, offering a dynamic hiking experience.

Q: Are there any specific trails that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, several trails offer views of waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e951900955/riera-de-castellnou-salt-de-la-tosca-moia-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riera de Castellnou, Salt de la Tosca, Moià – Circular Route&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice that allows you to experience the local scenery and a beautiful waterfall.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trailheads in Moià?
A: While Moià is well-connected, specific public transport options directly to all trailheads might be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules to Moià itself and then plan your hike, as many routes start directly from or near the town center.

Q: Are there parking facilities available for hikers in Moià?
A: Yes, Moià, as a regional center, typically offers parking facilities within the town. Many hiking routes start from Moià, making it convenient to park your vehicle before heading out on the trails.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Moià?
A: Moià offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly popular due to milder temperatures and vibrant natural colors. Summer can be warm, but the higher elevation and shaded forests provide some relief. Winter hikes are also possible, especially on easier routes.

In Moià is the Rafael Casanova Birthplace Museum. The jurist and Doctor of Law Rafael Casanova was born here in 1660. In addition to his biography, the museum also houses the municipal archives and the Moià Museum, which features archaeological collections, primarily from the Toll caves.
